title: rubus vestitus subsp. drejeri
intro: Rubus vestitus subsp. drejeri, also known as the Red-berried Raspberry or Drejer's Raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus vestitus plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Red-berried Raspberry include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and provide antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water, or as a dried extract, to utilize its medicinal properties and health benefits.
